summarization.a: The figure shows infrared absorbance spectra of B2O3 films grown by ALD using BCl3 and H2O at 40 °C after 1, 5, 10, and 15 cycles. The progressive increase in the B–O stretching and B–O–B bending bands reflects steady film growth with increasing number of ALD cycles.

a.question: How do the infrared spectra indicate growth of the B2O3 film during ALD?
a.answer: The increasing intensity of the B–O stretching band with each additional ALD cycle shows that more B–O bonds are being incorporated into the film. This steady growth in absorbance is consistent with layer-by-layer deposition during the ALD process.

a.question: Which spectral feature shows the most pronounced increase with cycle number?
What vibrational mode is associated with the feature near 700–800 cm⁻¹?
Do the spectra suggest continuous B2O3 film growth up to 15 ALD cycles?
